plugin_desc: Datacom DmOS-specific SNMP collection for switches (DMOS-SYSMON-MIB, IANA PEN 3709). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects Datacom private OIDs for device health: per-slot CPU load (cpuLoadOneMinuteActive) and system memory (used/total bytes). The DmOS SYSMON MIB only models CPU and memory subtrees; temperature/fan/power-supply objects exist only in the legacy DMswitch-MIB (incompatible with DmOS) and are not collected here (N/A). CPU utilization is averaged across slots; memory utilization is computed from used/total.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU load of the Datacom DmOS device control plane (DMOS-SYSMON-MIB cpuLoadOneMinuteActive, one-minute active percentage), averaged across CPU slots and normalized to 0-100. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_used
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of bytes currently in use by the Datacom DmOS system memory (DMOS-SYSMON-MIB memoryOneMinuteUsed). It helps administrators understand absolute memory consumption and identify when the device is under memory pressure.

metrics.name: device_memory_total
metrics.description: This metric reports the total size in bytes of the Datacom DmOS system memory (DMOS-SYSMON-MIB memoryOneMinuteTotal). Together with the used bytes it determines overall memory utilization.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the Datacom DmOS device, computed as used / total * 100. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
